channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: The Latest: Federal workers face mass confusion as Musk’s deadline to list accomplishments looms
item.description: Confusion and chaos loom as hundreds of thousands of federal employees begin their workweek Monday facing a deadline from President Donald Trump’s cost-cutting chief, Elon Musk, to explain their recent accomplishments or risk losing their jobs.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The young techies behind DOGE are a lightning rod for criticism but also a youth magnet for the GOP
item.description: A group of young engineers is playing a vital role in billionaire Trump adviser Elon Musk’s effort to slash the federal bureaucracy through his Department of Government Efficiency.
item.dc:creator: Christine Fernando, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The UN will vote to demand Russia pull troops out of Ukraine. But the US wants a softer approach
item.description: On the third anniversary of Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, the U.N. General Assembly is expected to vote on dueling resolutions.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump meets with French President Macron as uncertainty grows about US ties to Europe and Ukraine
item.description: President Donald Trump and French President Emmanuel Macron are starting their day by taking part in a virtual meeting with fellow leaders of the Group of Seven economies to discuss Russia's war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Trump administration is putting USAID staffers on leave worldwide and firing at least 1,600
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration moved its fast-paced dismantling of the U.S. Agency for International Development toward what appeared to be its final phases, telling all but a fraction of staffers worldwide that they were on leave as of Monday and notifying at least 1,600 of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas Lottery commissioner resigns as agency faces legislative scrutiny
item.description: Clark Smith, one of five members of the commission, stepped down Friday as lawmakers have raised questions about the role of lottery couriers and agency governance.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Ayden Runnels, KPRC 2

item.title: 2,000 USAID staff to be cut, others put on administrative leave
item.description: “There is no one left to do any work,” the USAID official said, requesting anonymity to protect against retaliation.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: 80 years ago Marines raised flag over Iwo Jima in iconic moment
item.description: On this day in 1945, six U.S. Marines raised the American flag over the island of Iwo Jima on the fourth day of what would become over a monthlong brutal battle.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mike Rowe says Trump policies will lead to short-term pain, but long-term gain
item.description: TV host Mike Rowe said that President Donald Trump's policies will be worth it in the long run, making the United States less reliant on adversaries such as China.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk gives all federal workers 48 hours to explain what they did last week or face consequences
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of federal workers have been given little more than 48 hours to explain what they accomplished over the last week, sparking confusion across key agencies as billionaire Elon Musk expands his drive to slash the size of federal government.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Many Americans don't trust the media to cover Trump: Survey
item.description: Most Americans in a new survey said they have little confidence that the news media can cover President Trump with fairness and accuracy.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: At CPAC, Argentina’s Milei explains his chainsaw methods and likens them to Musk’s DOGE
item.description: OXON HILL, Md. (AP) — Argentina’s President Javier Milei on Saturday urged an enthusiastic crowd of U.S. conservatives to keep supporting measures to shrink the size of their government, saying his methods are similar to those undertaken by President Donald Trump and close ally Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge largely blocks Trump‘s executive orders ending federal support for DEI programs
item.description: A U.S. district judge granted a preliminary injunction blocking the administration from terminating or changing federal contracts they consider equity-related.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Judge cancels NYC Mayor Eric Adams' trial and leaves corruption charges intact until mid-March
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Friday canceled the corruption trial for New York City Mayor Eric Adams and appointed counsel to advise the court about the Justice Department’s controversial request to drop charges against the Democrat. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Supreme Court won’t allow Trump to immediately fire head of whistleblower office
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday temporarily kept on the job the head of the federal agency that protects government whistleblowers, in its first word on the many legal fights over President Donald Trump’s second-term agenda.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Fired federal workers hunt for new jobs but struggle to replace their old ones
item.description: Job searches can be tough for anyone, but some of the federal workers flooding the market after widespread layoffs are finding it particularly tough.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sedensky, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge cancels trial for NYC Mayor Eric Adams but leaves corruption charges intact for now
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Friday cancelled the corruption trial for New York City Mayor Eric Adams and appointed counsel to advise him on how to handle the Justice Department’s controversial request to drop charges against the Democrat. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Gov. Hochul won't remove NYC Mayor Eric Adams, but pushes to limit powers
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City Mayor Eric Adams will remain in office as Gov. Kathy Hochul pushes to implement "certain guardrails" to help protect New Yorkers. Hochul cited the “democratic process” as why she chose not to remove Adams from office.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Lawmakers to Trump: Reverse cuts to 9/11 health care
item.description: New York's senate and congressional delegations agree that federal cuts to the World Trade Center Health Program will hurt survivors of and first responders to the Sept. 11 terrorist attacks.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, PIX 11

item.title: Senate adopts budget resolution after marathon overnight vote-a-rama
item.description: The Senate adopted a budget resolution Friday intended to serve as a blueprint to deliver the first part of President Trump’s agenda. Senators voted 52-48, mostly along party lines, on the resolution after a marathon overnight voting session. Sen. Rand Paul (R-Ky.) voted against the measure.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Department broadens Jan. 6 pardons to cover gun, drug-related charges
item.description: In a new development, the Department of Justice said Trump's pardon of Jan. 6 defendants should apply more broadly and include separate gun charges, as well.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dreisbach, MPR News

item.title: Proposed $500,000 fund would help victims of investment schemes in Nevada
item.description: Victims of investment schemes would be eligible for $25,000 from a fund managed by the Nevada Secretary of State's Office under a proposal presented in Carson City on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Planned medical research grant cuts worry Sarasota scientists
item.description: For more than 20 years, Sarasota-based biomedical research facility The Roskamp Institute has been focused on understanding and finding treatment, prevention, and early detection for Alzheimer's disease and other dementia, along with illnesses facing veterans.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: First CDC vaccine meeting under Trump administration is postponed
item.description: The panel of vaccine experts were supposed to hold their first meeting under the Trump administration in late February. It’s not clear when that meeting will now take place.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone, MPR News

item.title: Bill that would make more attorney general data public fails in Minnesota House vote
item.description: House Republicans pushed for a vote on a bill to require the attorney general’s office, occupied now by DFLer Keith Ellison, to be more forthcoming with records. Democrats framed it as a partisan measure.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Frey vetoes council move toward a pedestrian plaza at George Floyd Square
item.description: Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey has vetoed the City Council’s plan to explore building a pedestrian-only mall at George Floyd Square, the intersection where an officer killed Floyd in 2020.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: A military vet and a scientist were securing America’s food system. Trump fired them
item.description: More than 10,000 federal employees who had yet to complete their probationary periods have been fired by the Trump administration, including those who work to protect American agriculture.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Democrats look to enshrine right to IVF in state law
item.description: Nevada Democrats introduced a proposal Wednesday to create a state law protecting the right to in vitro fertilization (IVF).
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gov. Gavin Newsom proposes $125M mortgage relief program to help victims of recent natural disasters
item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom on Wednesday proposed a new $125 million package to help residents whose homes were destroyed or damaged by recent natural disasters, including the recent wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Emotional testimony for 'Jaya's Law' argues for tougher penalties for wrong-way drivers
item.description: A Las Vegas family made an emotional plea to state lawmakers on Tuesday, telling the story of 3-year-old Jaya Brooks, who was killed in a crash involving a wrong-way driver in December 2023.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida cabinet assumes new role, sparking criticism over privacy and power
item.description: After a month-long feud over who would be the state's chief immigration enforcement officer, state lawmakers passed a bill package that grants not one, but four elected officials with that task.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump signals 25 percent tariffs coming on cars, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday indicated he would unveil tariffs on imports of automobiles, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals in early April, starting at 25 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republican introduces bill to make Trump's birthday a federal holiday
item.description: The Trump’s Birthday and Flag Day Holiday Establishment Act proposes the recognition of both Trump’s birthday and Flag Day, celebrated on June 14, as federal public holidays.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, Fox 5

item.title: Social Security head steps down over DOGE data access: Sources
item.description: King's exit from the administration is one of several departures of high-ranking officials concerned about DOGE staffers' potential unlawful access to private taxpayer information.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New Florida Attorney General sworn in, first priority illegal immigration
item.description: Florida’s new Attorney General has been sworn in and he's hitting the ground, running. After taking the oath of office, James Uthmeier stood with Gov. Ron DeSantis and the cabinet holding an emergency meeting to implement immigration enforcement measures.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Muller, WFLA, NBC
